commandsRun.command: cd "/Users/lubomirmolin/PhpstormProjects/codex-mobile-companion/apps/mobile" && flutter test integration_test/reconnect_offline_cache_test.dart --plain-name "reconnect restores trusted session, selected thread, approvals, and repo context without duplication" -r expanded
commandsRun.observation: Passed; the integration harness restored the trusted session after a reconnect retry, reopened selected thread-123, kept live and catch-up items deduplicated, restored approval-restore exactly once, and preserved repo context for codex-mobile-companion on master/origin at /workspace/codex-mobile-companion while resubscribing the live stream without re-pairing.

assertions.title: Reconnect restores auth, subscriptions, active thread, approvals, and repo context without re-pairing

steps.action: Inspect the reconnect/offline integration harness to identify the narrowest user-surface test that exercises the full restore bundle.
steps.expected: A targeted integration test should cover trusted-session restore, selected thread restore, approval restoration, repo-context restoration, resubscription, and deduplication without a new pairing flow.
steps.observed: The minimal matching test is integration_test/reconnect_offline_cache_test.dart :: reconnect restores trusted session, selected thread, approvals, and repo context without duplication.

steps.action: Run the targeted Flutter integration test with --plain-name for the reconnect restore case.
steps.expected: The trusted session should recover after reconnect, the selected thread should reopen, approvals and repo context should restore, live subscriptions should resume, and restored state should remain deduplicated without re-pairing.
steps.observed: The test passed: the UI recovered from Bridge disconnected to Paired with Codex Mobile Companion, reopened thread-123, restored one live item and one catch-up item without duplication, restored approval-restore with pendingCount 1, preserved repository=codex-mobile-companion branch=master remote=origin workspace=/workspace/codex-mobile-companion, and resubscribed/fetched approvals at least twice.

evidence.consoleErrors: not applicable on the Flutter integration-test surface; evidence is captured via the serialized Flutter test log
evidence.network: not applicable; the integration harness uses fake bridge APIs and a fake live stream to drive the real Flutter user surface

frictions: The flutter-mobile-integration-tests surface provides serialized Flutter test logs instead of standalone screenshots/video/websocket captures, so this rerun relies on the passing targeted integration log plus source-reviewed harness assertions.
Even the single targeted integration test rebuilt and reinstalled the debug app before execution, adding about 24 seconds of setup time to the rerun.
summary: Retested 1 assertion: VAL-CROSS-003 passed via the targeted reconnect_offline_cache integration harness.
